summary: Azure DNS is Microsoft's cloud-based DNS hosting and resolution service that translates domain names to IP addresses using Azure infrastructure. It supports both public internet-facing DNS domains and private DNS zones within virtual networks. It also includes traffic load balancing, security policy filtering, and hybrid on-premises integration via the DNS Private Resolver.

key_concepts.explanation: A hosting service for internet-facing DNS domains, letting you manage DNS records using the same Azure tools, credentials, and billing as other Azure resources.
A DNS service scoped to your virtual networks that resolves domain names internally without requiring a custom DNS server VM.
A managed service that allows on-premises networks to query Azure Private DNS zones (and vice versa) without deploying VM-based DNS servers.
A DNS-based load balancer that distributes traffic across global Azure regions for public-facing applications.
Filters and logs DNS queries at the virtual network level and blocks known malicious domains using Microsoft's threat intelligence feed (MSRC).
A feature of Azure Private DNS that automatically registers DNS records for VMs in a linked virtual network.

exam_focus_points: Azure Public DNS hosts internet-facing domains; Azure Private DNS handles name resolution within virtual networks
Azure DNS Private Resolver enables hybrid DNS resolution between on-premises and Azure without VM-based DNS servers
Traffic Manager is DNS-based and operates at the global level for load balancing public applications
Private DNS zones support autoregistration of VM hostnames in linked virtual networks
DNS Security Policy filters queries using Microsoft threat intelligence and operates at the virtual network level
Azure DNS does not register domain names — it only hosts and resolves them; you must purchase domains separately
similar_concepts: Azure Traffic Manager (DNS-based load balancing vs. DNS hosting)
Azure Private Link (often integrated with Private DNS but serves a different purpose)
Custom DNS servers on VMs (replaced by Azure DNS Private Resolver in hybrid scenarios)
Azure Front Door (application-layer load balancing vs. DNS-layer)
common_mistakes: Confusing Azure DNS (hosting/resolution) with domain registration — Azure DNS does not sell or register domain names
Using VM-based DNS forwarders for hybrid resolution instead of the managed Azure DNS Private Resolver
Confusing Azure Private DNS (VNet-scoped resolution) with Azure Public DNS (internet-facing)
Assuming Traffic Manager is a network load balancer — it is DNS-based and works at the DNS query level, not the packet level
Forgetting that Private DNS zones must be linked to a virtual network before resolution or autoregistration works
